title: Fever / Suspected infection

presenting_history.text: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped suspected-infection, fever, source, duration, and rigors fields.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports suspected-infection and fever presentation documentation, source symptoms, risk factors, and recognition that sepsis can present with nonspecific illness.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.2 — recognition, suspected infection, source, and risk factors

red_flags.text: Supports emergency transfer or escalation for high-risk presentations and documented monitoring, warning signs, and how to seek care for lower-risk presentations.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped escalation, return, and warning-information documentation.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports emergency transfer or escalation for high-risk presentations and documented monitoring, warning signs, and how to seek care for lower-risk presentations.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 1.7 — emergency transfer, monitoring, and information

examination.text: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped observations, source examination, urine-output, and general-assessment fields.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports temperature, heart rate, respiratory rate, blood pressure, consciousness, oxygen saturation, skin signs, urine output, fever or rigors, and examination for the suspected infection source.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 1.3 — history, observations, circulation, respiration, skin, urine output, and source examination

investigations.text: Supports clinician-selected blood and microbiology testing, urinalysis, chest radiography, and abdominal or pelvic imaging to identify an infection source.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped blood, microbiology, urine, chest-X-ray, and imaging review.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-selected blood and microbiology testing, urinalysis, chest radiography, and abdominal or pelvic imaging to identify an infection source.

source.title: Suspected sepsis in people aged 16 or over: recognition, assessment and early management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.8 and 1.11 — source identification and investigations

safety_netting.text: Supports confusion, respiratory compromise, hypotension, tachycardia, oliguria, temperature abnormality, non-blanching or mottled skin, and immunosuppression as assessed risk features.
safety_netting.rationale: Supports mapped confusion, hypotension, respiratory, rash, immunocompromise, and oliguria warning fields.
safety_netting.evidence_extract: Supports confusion, respiratory compromise, hypotension, tachycardia, oliguria, temperature abnormality, non-blanching or mottled skin, and immunosuppression as assessed risk features.

source_limitations: Children, pregnancy, neck stiffness, broad “temperature” matching, hydration assessment, and the history draft containing neck-stiffness text remain unsupported.
Supportive care, hydration advice, antipyretics, antibiotics, and fixed follow-up remain unsupported and clinician-led.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
